# Extracao: transcription_mp4_01-Nostalgia

- **Fonte:** transcription_mp4_01-Nostalgia.txt
- **Tipo:** TXT
- **Tamanho:** 0 MB

---

Nostalgia. It's hard to explain why nostalgia makes people laugh, but it really does. When I tell a story about being stung by a bee for the first time as a kid and having an allergic reaction, I talk about how confusing the allergic reaction is to me, because I've never seen an allergic reaction before, because it's the 1980s, and no one is allergic to anything. I eat peanut butter sandwiches on gluten-packed bread baked in asbestos factories. Why would I be allergic to a bee sting? It makes people laugh every time. Anytime you can talk about something from the past and remind people of the way we used to do things, it'll be funny. When I talk about the VCR that we had in our house, actually VCR is enough to make people laugh, but when I mentioned that the remote control was attached to the VCR by a cord and that the VCR weighed about 500 pounds, which allowed me to trip my brother with the cord as he passed through the living room, always makes people laugh. In business, nostalgia is really helpful because oftentimes the products and services that you're making, that you're providing your customers, are improvements upon things that once existed in the past. So when I was working with Slack and we were trying to create a marketing plan for the digital HQ, the headquarters they were building on the internet, we were able to do that. We were able to talk about the way work used to be done. We made jokes about cubicle farms in the 1990s and open office plans in the 2000s, those plans that forced introverts to don headphones and stare aggressively at their screens, lest they'd be forced to make eye contact with their coworkers. We talked about the Googleplexes, the places that became campuses for workplaces, so that you could bring your family to work and eat food at work and never leave work. All of these things made people laugh because they were nostalgic. They gave people an opportunity to look into the past and notice the absurdity of the past, the crazy ways we used to do things. And that is why nostalgia makes people laugh and why it's such an easy way to inject humor into your stories.
